content: Fibonacci levels have no fundamental economic basis; their effectiveness relies entirely on self-fulfilling prophecy. With infinite retracement levels possible, selecting Fibonacci ratios is arbitrary. Studies show price action at Fib levels is no different than at random levels. The 'golden ratio' appears throughout nature but has no proven relevance to market psychology. Different analysts draw Fibs from different swing points, creating inconsistency. Any apparent success is confirmation bias and data mining.
